extra.message: Student.create() uses Python % string formatting to insert the 'name' parameter directly into the SQL INSERT statement with VALUES ('%(name)s'). User input from request.post() at views.py:55-57 flows to this sink without any sanitization or validation. This allows SQL injection.

Stored XSS: review.review_text is rendered without the |e escape filter. User-supplied review text (views.py:121) is stored in the database via Review.create() and then rendered unescaped on the course page. With autoescape=False (app.py:35), any HTML/JavaScript in the review text will be executed in browsers of all visitors to the course page.

Stored XSS: course.title and course.description are rendered without the |e escape filter. Course data is created from user POST input (views.py:89-90) and rendered unescaped. With autoescape=False (app.py:35), this enables stored XSS.

Stored XSS: course.title and course.description are rendered without the |e escape filter in the courses listing. Since courses are created from unvalidated user POST data (views.py:89-90) with no authentication requirement, any unauthenticated user can inject XSS payloads into course names that execute for all visitors.

Stored XSS: Student name {{ name }} is rendered without the |e escape filter. Student names are created from user POST data (views.py:57) and rendered unescaped. With autoescape=False (app.py:35), this enables stored XSS.

Jinja2 autoescape is explicitly set to False, disabling HTML output escaping globally. This is the root cause enabling all XSS vulnerabilities in templates. Every template output that doesn't use the |e filter is vulnerable. Several templates inconsistently use |e (e.g., student.jinja2:3, review.jinja2:10) but many others do not.

cwe: CWE-116: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output

extra.message: Missing authorization: The POST handler for /students/ has no @authorize decorator and no authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can send a POST request to create students. Although the template hides the form from unauthenticated users (students.jinja2:21), the server-side enforcement is absent.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authorization: The POST handler for /courses/ has no @authorize decorator and no authentication or admin check. Any unauthenticated user can create courses by directly POST-ing to this endpoint. The template restricts the form to admin users (courses.jinja2:25), but server-side enforcement is absent.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authorization: The POST handler for /courses/{course_id}/review has no @authorize decorator and no authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can submit course reviews. With autoescape disabled, this enables stored XSS that affects all visitors to the course page.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authorization: The evaluate() view has no @authorize decorator and no authentication or admin check. Any unauthenticated user can assign marks to any student for any course by directly POST-ing to /students/{student_id}/evaluate/{course_id}. The template restricts the form to admins (course.jinja2:41), but server-side enforcement is absent.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Hardcoded database credentials (user: postgres, password: postgres) are stored in plaintext in the configuration file committed to the repository. These credentials provide direct access to the PostgreSQL database.

MD5 is used for password hashing: self.pwd_hash == md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest(). MD5 is a broken cryptographic hash algorithm unsuitable for password storage. It is vulnerable to rainbow table attacks, collisions, and fast brute-force. Passwords are also stored as raw MD5 hashes in the database (see migrations/001-fixtures.sql:10-12).

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: The CSRF protection middleware (csrf_middleware) is commented out. Although templates generate CSRF tokens via csrf_processor (jinja2.py:8-16), the server-side validation is disabled. All POST endpoints (login, create student, create course, evaluate, review) are vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks.

Session cookies are configured with httponly=False, making them accessible to JavaScript via document.cookie. Combined with the XSS vulnerabilities from autoescape=False, an attacker can steal session cookies and hijack user sessions.

The 500 error page dumps the full internal state of exception objects: 
{{ error.__dict__ }}
. This exposes internal application details (stack traces, file paths, database information, configuration values) to end users, which can aid attackers in reconnaissance.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: Stored XSS: student.name is rendered without the |e escape filter on this error page. While student.name is escaped on line 9 with |e, it is not escaped in the 'Back to student' link text on line 25. With autoescape=False, if an attacker has injected HTML into a student name (via the SQL injection vulnerability), it will execute here.

Input to Student.create() (data['name']) from request.post() is used directly without validation against the STUDENT_SCHEMA defined in schema/forms.py:12-14. The schema is defined but never applied in the view handler, meaning invalid or malicious input of arbitrary length passes through unchecked.
